meaning: n.牺牲,献身,祭品,供奉v.牺牲,献出,献祭,供奉
examples: Should motherhood necessarily mean sacrifice and self-denial?
He was willing to sacrifice his happiness on the altar of fame.
I thanked my parents for all their self-sacrifice on my behalf.
Professor BOB LINDEN (Harvard University): In their mind, reform doesn't mean sacrifice on their part.
An unwillingness to sacrifice performance for styling is the new mantra at the 98-year-old company.
